title: Inspect /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public for how Kernel and /vote are currently positioned. Focus on CODEX.md, README.md, .codex/prompts/kernel.md, .codex/prompts/vote.md, scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh, scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh, scripts/local/launchers/*. Return the min...
summary_text: This plan is mostly sound, but add hardening to avoid hidden data loss/duplication. 1. Refined safe workflow (concise) 1. Save a status snapshot first (optional but useful): `git -C "$repo" status --short`, `git -C "$repo" rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`. 2. Stash with explicit scope: - If you want only tracked+unstaged/staged: `git stash push -m "archival snapshot $(date -Iseconds)"`. - If you need untracked too: add `-u` (`-a` only if you explicitly want ignored files too). 3. Create branch and fresh worktree: - `git -C "$repo" branch archival/<ts>` (or `git switch -c archival/<ts>` in original). - `git worktree add -B archival/<ts> ../fugue-archival-<ts>`. 4. In new worktree, apply stash: - `git -C ../fugue-archival-<ts> stash apply` (or `pop` if you want to consume it). 5. Commit snapshot once verified clean: - `git add -A && git commit -m "chore: archive dirty-worktree snapshot"`. 6. Return original clean: - `git -C "$repo" stash drop` (or `drop` only if applied with `apply`; if `pop`, it is already removed). - `git -C "$repo" status --short` should be clean. 2. Highest-value safety pitfalls 1. Scope mismatch in stash is the biggest risk. - If you use `git stash` without `-u`, untracked changes are dropped from archival. - If you use `-a`, ignored sensitive files can be unintentionally archived. 2. Applying a large/legacy stash into a fresh worktree can conflict. - Fix: pause after `git stash apply` and resolve before commit; do not force commit on conflict. 3. Branch/worktree naming collisions can create wrong target checkouts. - Use timestamped, unique `archival/<date>-<shortsha>` names and verify `git worktree list`. 4. “Keep original clean” can be accidentally violated if stash pop is done in the original by mistake. - Use separate terminal/paths and explicit `-C` workdir on every command. 5. “Fresh worktree” implies you should use `git worktree` (not just a new branch in same worktree), otherwise you don’t get true isolation. 3. Suggested refinements - Prefer `git stash push --include-untracked` only when you explicitly want untracked files. - Add a pre-flight check that the stash is present before creating/using new worktree. - Add a final verification commit: `git -C ../fugue-archival-<ts> rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`, `git -C ../fugue-archival-<ts> status --short`, and one `git show --stat --oneline --check --max-count=1` after commit.
